name: Almond-Apricot Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces
3 1/2 oz. imported white chocolate (such as Lindt), cut into pieces (white chocolate chips probably be an ok substitution)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 18 x 12 x 1-inch baking sheet with foil. Butter and flour the foil.
2. Combine the first six ingredients in a food processor. Process until a fine meal forms.
3. Add white chocolate and process until finely chopped.
4. Add toasted almonds and chop coarsely, using 6 to 8 on/off turns.
5. Beat eggs, brandy, and extract together in a large bowl until blended.
6. Add the flour mixture and apricots and stir until moist dough forms.
7. Drop dough by spoonfuls into three 12-inch-long strips on the prepared sheet, spacing evenly.
8. Moisten fingertips and shape each dough strip into a 2-inch-wide log.
9. Refrigerate the logs until firm, about 30 minutes.
10. Position the rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
11. Bake the logs until golden, about 30 minutes. Transfer the sheet to the rack and cool completely.
12. Reduce heat to 300 degrees F (150 degrees C). Cut the logs from the sides of the pan if necessary.
13. Transfer each log to a work surface. Using a heavy, sharp knife, cut each log crosswise into 3/4-inch-wide slices.
14. Arrange half of the cookies, cut side down, on a baking sheet. Bake for 10 minutes.
15. Gently turn the cookies over and bake an additional 10 minutes longer.
16. Transfer the cookies to racks. Repeat baking with the remaining cookies.
17. Cool the cookies completely.
This recipe can be prepared up to 2 weeks in advance. Store the biscuits in an airtight container at room temperature.
